files_inspected: 0DTE (full tree manifest, 305 tracked files) via git ls-tree -r
files_deferred: All System A production code. Phase 0 pins and inventories source only; migration begins in Phase 1 (spec §63).

tests.name: migration map source-existence validation
tests.detail: Every source path referenced in spec §62 provisional map confirmed present in the pinned tree; 0 missing, 0 fabricated.

known_gaps: No parity fixtures or expected-output captures exist yet; baseline/fixtures/ and baseline/expected_outputs/ are created empty and populated starting Phase 1 (spec §63 Phase 1 'initial parity fixtures').
Migration map is validated for source EXISTENCE only; behavioral correctness of each mapping is proven per-phase via parity tests (spec §65).

System A carries its own honest gaps (HANDOFF.md §5): stale intraday OI-GEX, CVD proxy zero on mid-bar closes, fixed non-adaptive scales, observation-only signal tranche with no gate power. Carried forward so later phases do not misread them as migration defects.
Clone is shallow (--depth 1); full history requires `git fetch --unshallow` before blame/log/bisect-dependent parity work.
rollback: Phase 0 introduces no runtime code and changes no System A source; it is documentation, a baseline pin, and manifests only.
To revert: `git revert` (or drop) the Phase 0 commit on branch claude/spy-der-phase-0-handoff-0ctzxv. Removing baseline/system_a.lock.json unpins the baseline with no runtime effect.
The System A pin is immutable by hash; if upstream rewrites/removes commit de4a6e7, the stored hashes stop reproducing and Phase 0 must be re-run against a new pin (fail-closed, spec §5).
next_phase: Phase 1 — Package and canonical ingestion foundation (spec §63): spy_der package normalization, common + market contracts, market calendar, feed provenance/freshness, canonical snapshot assembler, System A snapshot adapter, deterministic IDs, initial parity fixtures.
